entries.definition:
h–r–r heat, thirst, to become hot, to intensify; volcanic rocky
land; free person, to liberate, to set free; the best of anything, to
dedicate to the service of God; silk, to set right. Of this root, six
forms occur 15 times in the Qur’an: تَحْرِير taḥrīr five times; مُحَرَّر
muḥarrar once; حُرّ ḥurr twice; حَرّ ḥarr three times; حَرُور ḥarūr
once and حَرِير ḥarīr three times.

تَحْرِير taḥrīr [v. n.] liberating, setting free *(4:92) تَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ

199
